items.quote: You can't use up creativity. The more you use, the more you have.
items.author: Maya Angelou

items.quote: Absorb what is useful, discard what is not, add what is uniquely your own.
items.author: Bruce Lee

items.quote: New ideas need old buildings.
items.author: Jane Jacobs

items.quote: The world is before you and you need not take it or leave it as it was when you came in.
items.author: James Baldwin

items.quote: It is good to have an end to journey toward; but it is the journey that matters, in the end.
items.author: Ursula K. Le Guin

items.quote: If you want to fly, you have to give up the things that weigh you down.
items.author: Toni Morrison

items.quote: Tell me, what is it you plan to do with your one wild and precious life?
items.author: Mary Oliver

items.quote: All that you touch you change. All that you change changes you.
items.author: Octavia E. Butler

items.quote: Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one's courage.
items.author: Anais Nin

items.quote: Rarely, if ever, are any of us healed in isolation. Healing is an act of communion.
items.author: bell hooks

items.quote: Creativity takes courage.
items.author: Henri Matisse

items.quote: Life is either a daring adventure or nothing at all.
items.author: Helen Keller

items.quote: I am not what happened to me, I am what I choose to become.
items.author: Carl Jung

items.quote: No matter how isolated you are and how lonely you feel, if you do your work truly and conscientiously, unknown friends will come and seek you.
items.author: Carl Jung

items.quote: Religious suffering is, at one and the same time, the expression of real suffering and a protest against real suffering. Religion is the sigh of the oppressed creature, the heart of a heartless world, and the soul of soulless conditions. It is the opium of the people.
items.author: Karl Marx

items.quote: The best life is the one in which the creative impulses play the largest part and the possessive impulses the smallest.
items.author: Bertrand Russell

items.quote: A man's character is seen or tested when he has everything, and a woman's character is tested when her man has nothing.
items.author: Anon

items.quote: Talent hits a target no one else can hit; genius hits a target no one else can see.
items.author: Arthur Schopenhauer

items.quote: For the more a man has in himself, the less he will want from other people, the less, indeed, other people can be to him. This is why a high degree of intellect tends to make a man unsocial. True.
items.author: Arthur Schopenhauer

items.quote: Don’t waste time arguing with idiots. They’ll drag you down to their level and they’ll beat you with experience.
items.author: Mark Twain

items.quote: Prejudice is a great time saver. You can form opinions without having to get the facts.
items.author: E. B. White

items.quote: From the ashes of disaster grow the roses of success.
items.author: Caractacus Potts
